title: Trace Viewer Session Bundle (tmtrace/v1)
description: v1 of the .tmtrace multi-trace Trace Viewer session bundle, also used by the Replay tab (v3.7.0) as a degenerate single-source case (sources: [1], most multi-trace fields empty). Path-reference only for .asc and DBC files; recordings are NOT embedded. JSON encoding is UTF-8 with BOM, written atomically (tmp + rename) by TraceSessionLibrary.Save. Round-tripped by TraceSessionLibrary.Load. See docs/release-notes-v3.5.0.md for the format design, docs/release-notes-v3.6.0.md for the v1 restore-Color/DisplayName semantics, docs/release-notes-v3.6.4.md for the v3.6.4 hash-based .asc relocation feature, and docs/release-notes-v3.7.0.md for the v3.7.0 Replay tab adoption (each BundleSourceDto may carry an optional 'contentHash' SHA-256 fingerprint; the playback envelope carries an optional 'replayCanIdFilterText' field for the Replay tab's CAN-ID filter; if the recorded 'path' is missing at load time the consumer may search a user-known directory list for a relocated copy whose hash matches). Forward-compat: adding new top-level or nested fields is non-breaking (System.Text.Json ignores unknown keys on deserialize); the schema sets additionalProperties=true to allow the producer to write fields a strict consumer might not know about. Renaming or removing fields IS a breaking change and requires a schema bump.

version.description: Bundle format version. Constrained to 1; bumping requires a schema-versioned file (e.g. tmtrace-v2.schema.json).

schema.description: Schema identifier. Constrained to 'tmtrace/v1' for this file; any other value means a different schema and this validator should reject.

savedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) of when the bundle was written. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffffffZ. May carry sub-second precision depending on producer.

appVersion.description: AssemblyInformationalVersion of the writer (stripped of any '+git<sha>' suffix). Pure documentation; consumers should not branch on this value.

dbcPath.description: Absolute or relative path to the .dbc file. May be empty if no DBC was loaded. Path-reference only — the DBC contents are NOT embedded.

globalCanIdFilter.description: Per-application CAN-ID filter applied across all sources in the bundle. Format: comma-separated hex IDs or ranges (see CanIdListParser in the project). Empty string means 'no filter'.

playback.description: Playback transport state (master source, loop, speed, scrubber, time bounds). Optional — bundles saved without playback (e.g. fresh user-save of a static session) may omit this key entirely; consumers must treat null/missing as 'playback not captured, use defaults'.

sources.description: Array of loaded .asc traces in the bundle. Order is producer-chosen (typically insertion order).

viewports.description: Per-series chart viewport (X-axis range, focus, collapse). Keyed by EffectiveKey = '<SourceId>.<SignalKey>'. Order is producer-chosen.

BundleSourceDto.description: One loaded trace in the saved session.

sourceId.description: Producer-assigned unique identifier for this source. Generated as a GUID string by the registry on LoadAsync; consumers should NOT assume any particular format beyond uniqueness within the bundle's sources array.

displayName.description: User-visible label shown in the Trace Viewer strip. If empty or matching the path's filename, the registry's default (filename without extension) is used instead — see ApplySnapshotAsync fallback at TraceViewerViewModel.cs.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the .asc recording. Path-reference only — the recording is NOT embedded. May not exist on the consumer's filesystem; the consumer must handle missing files gracefully (skip with warning).

colorA.description: Alpha channel of the strip color (0-255). Bytes stored individually (not as packed ARGB int) for readability.

strokeStyle.description: OxyPlot LineStyle name (Solid, Dash, Dot, DashDot, DashDashDot, DashDotDot, LongDash, LongDashDot, LongDashDotDot, None, Hidden, Automatic). Producer usually emits 'Solid' for default; consumer should tolerate unknown values by falling back to Solid.

canIdFilter.description: Per-source CAN-ID filter (overrides the globalCanIdFilter for this source). Same format as globalCanIdFilter; empty string means 'inherit global'.

contentHash.description: v3.6.4 PATCH: lowercase hex SHA-256 (FIPS 180-4) of the .asc recording at 'path'. Enables hash-based relocation when the user moves or renames the .asc after the bundle was saved. Empty string means 'hash unknown' (the bundle was saved by v3.6.0-v3.6.3, or hashing was unavailable at save time); the consumer falls back to the existing path-only resolution. The optional '?' in the regex pattern matches the empty-string case.

BundlePlaybackDto.description: Playback transport state at save time. Consumers must restore to a paused/stopped cursor; auto-resume on app restart is a UX anti-pattern.

masterSourceId.description: SourceId of the trace driving playback master clock. Empty string means 'no master set'.

loop.description: Whether playback wraps at the end.

speed.description: Playback rate multiplier (1.0 = real-time). Negative values mean reverse playback (consumer-specific).

scrubberValue.description: Scrubber position in trace-relative seconds (NOT a DateTime — relative to the first frame of the master source).

startTimestamp.description: Lower bound of the visible trace range, or null for 'no lower bound'. Same units as scrubberValue.

endTimestamp.description: Upper bound of the visible trace range, or null for 'no upper bound'. Same units as scrubberValue.

replayCanIdFilterText.description: v3.7.0 MINOR: Replay-tab CAN-ID filter text (free-form, comma- or whitespace-separated decimal/hex tokens). Persisted alongside the playback envelope because the filter is a transport-state concern for the Replay tab (it affects what frames are emitted), not a per-source display concern. The Trace Viewer keeps its filter on BundleSourceDto.canIdFilter (per-source) and TraceSessionBundleDto.globalCanIdFilter (global); the Replay tab has only the single source, so a sibling field on the playback envelope is the right shape. Default = empty string (= no filter). Forward-compat: deserializers that do not know this field get an empty string, which matches 'no filter' and is a safe no-op.

bookmarks.description: Replay-tab bookmarks — user-captured timestamps (Ctrl+B / the + Bookmark toolbar button) for quick re-navigation. Each BookmarkDto is a (id, timestamp, label) tuple. Empty array = no bookmarks (default). Null deserializes as empty; producers SHOULD emit [] for forward-compat with v3.7.x readers.

loopRegions.description: Named playback windows. When Loop is true AND this array is non-empty, the FIRST region overrides StartTimestamp/EndTimestamp for wrap-around. Empty array = legacy single-region behavior. Partial: regions seek to their Start on activation but do NOT yet rewind at End (full A/B loop is a future enhancement). Null deserializes as empty; producers SHOULD emit [] for forward-compat with v3.7.x readers.

BookmarkDto.description: v3.8.0 MINOR: a single Replay-tab bookmark. Id is a 32-char hex GUID; timestamp is trace-relative seconds; label is optional free-form text.

id.description: 32-char hex GUID (no dashes) for unique identification across saves.

timestamp.description: Trace-relative seconds (NOT a DateTime — relative to the first frame of the loaded .asc).

label.description: Optional free-form label. Empty / missing = no label.

LoopRegionDto.description: v3.8.0 MINOR: a named playback window — the cursor wraps to 'start' when reaching 'end' AND Loop is true. See BundlePlaybackDto.loopRegions precedence rule. Empty label = no label.

id.description: 32-char hex GUID (no dashes) for unique identification across saves.

start.description: Lower bound of the region, in trace-relative seconds.

end.description: Upper bound of the region, in trace-relative seconds.

BundleViewportDto.description: Per-series chart viewport — X-axis range plus focus/collapse flags. The strip's chart rebuilds these after RebuildSignalsCore (otherwise per-axis writes land on stale PlotModels and SyncYAxes overwrites them).

effectiveKey.description: Composite key '<SourceId>.<SignalKey>'. Disambiguates viewports across multi-trace sessions where the same signal name may appear in multiple sources.

xMin.description: Left edge of the X-axis (trace-relative seconds).

xMax.description: Right edge of the X-axis (trace-relative seconds).

isFocused.description: Whether this series is the focused row (highlighted, sync-pinned).

isCollapsed.description: Whether the series row is collapsed in the chart strip (chart hidden, only header visible).
